summary: Draft PR, closed unmerged, that attempted a large HSD JPEG decomp/refactor across sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C. It replaced much raw M2C/jmp-buffer-offset style code with an HSDJpegWork work-buffer struct, clearer DCT/IDCT-style transform loops, split lookup tables, revised prototypes, and a local JPEG bit-emission macro. The direction was useful for readability and semantic discovery, but decomp.dev reported broken matches and sizable regressions, especially hsd_3B34 .data and hsd_803B5C2C, so this PR is best treated as a cautionary/idea source rather than a landed matching fix.

key_files.role: Main HSD JPEG encoder-side rewrite: table declarations, hsd_803B3CD8/hsd_803B4D64/hsd_803B51C8 work, bit emission, and use of HSDJpegWork over raw offsets.
Added HSDJpegWork, changed several prototypes, and exposed helper symbols such as fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, and fn_803B6820.
Decoder-side/type cleanup and transform/color-conversion rewrites using HSDJpegWork and clearer loops.

what_changed: Modified 3 files with 801 added lines and 2211 deleted lines; PR body was absent and there were no human review comments in the slice.

In hsd_3B34.c, added hsd_3A94.h and __mem.h, removed placeholder/Gecko_setjmp/string includes, introduced static data for hsd_803B5C2C, and changed several extern declarations including hsd_804D79BC from u8* to s32.

Reorganized JPEG-related tables: lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, lbl_80431638, lbl_80431678, lbl_80431690, lbl_8043169C, and lbl_804316B4 were declared explicitly near the top of hsd_3B34.c; lbl_803B9670 changed from a fixed-size array to an unsized const array.
Added HSDJpegWork in hsd_3B34.h with pad_000[0x118], luma[256], cb[64], cr[64], tmp[64], and pred[3], then used it as a typed view over hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70.
Rewrote transform helpers fn_803B376C and fn_803B61B4 with named f32 temporaries and 8-iteration row/column loops, followed by a 64-element right-shift pass.
Reworked hsd_803B3CD8 around JPEG DC/AC Huffman emission, using dc_code/dc_size/ac_code/ac_size lookup pointers and a local HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS macro to replace repeated manual byte-emission blocks.
Revised hsd_803B51C8 to use HSDJpegWork state, __setjmp on hsd_804D2648, hsd_803B3344/hsd_803B3398 byte writes, JPEG marker output, comment sizing from lbl_803B9670, and final EOI emission.

In hsd_3B5C.c, changed signatures including hsd_803B5C4C(s32)->int, hsd_803B5D70(s32,s32)->u8, hsd_803B5EA0(s32)->void hsd_803B5EA0(int), and hsd_803B6BE4(char*,s32,void*)->int hsd_803B6BE4(char*,int,void*).
Made previously static helpers fn_803B61B4 and fn_803B6820 non-static and declared them in hsd_3B34.h.
decomp.dev reported two .sdata2 improvements but also broken matches and many regressions, and the PR was closed without merging.
smart_moves: Introducing HSDJpegWork was a useful semantic step: it replaced opaque offsets like hsd_804D2648 + 0x118/0x518/0x618/0x718/0x818 with fields such as luma, cb, cr, tmp, and pred.
The local HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS macro captured repeated JPEG bit-buffer behavior, including byte flushing and 0xFF byte stuffing, making the intended control flow easier to audit.

The transform rewrites used named math variables and row/column passes instead of long blocks of temp_r/temp_f decompiler output, which is valuable for understanding DCT/IDCT code even if it did not match here.
Keeping address-derived labels like lbl_80431638 and function names like fn_803B61B4 while adding semantics avoided premature invented names.
The header collected the newly inferred work-buffer structure and helper prototypes in one place, which is helpful for cross-file hsd_3B34/hsd_3B5C coordination.
decomp_lessons: Typed work-buffer structs are powerful but risky: every field offset must be verified before replacing raw M2C_FIELD accesses, especially when the same storage is also used as a jmp buffer.

Large JPEG lookup tables are match-sensitive. Moving declarations, changing staticness, splitting lbl_80430C80 into smaller/table-adjacent arrays, or changing array sizes can regress .data even when the values look correct.

Readable loops can hurt MWCC matching when the original codegen was unrolled or depended on specific temporary lifetimes; this PR's loop cleanup coincided with regressions in hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B51C8, fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, and fn_803B6820.
Prototype cleanup must be tied to ABI/codegen evidence. Changes like s32->int, u32->s32, and s32 return->u8 return may be semantically plausible but can alter matching.
When a PR touches both code and data layout, split the work into smaller match-checkable steps: first table placement/staticness, then struct overlays, then algorithmic cleanup.
Use bot reports as hard evidence. Here, the readability improvements did not outweigh the reported broken hsd_3B34 .data and hsd_803B5C2C matches.
naming_conventions: HSDJpegWork fields were named descriptively: luma, cb, cr, tmp, pred, with pad_000 preserving unknown leading layout.
Unknown helper functions retained address-style fn_ names: fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820.
Existing address-based data labels were preserved: lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, lbl_80431638, lbl_80431678, lbl_80431690, lbl_8043169C, lbl_804316B4.
Header prototypes continued the project convention of address comments such as /* 3B3408 */ and /* 3B61B4 */ before declarations.
A local all-caps macro name, HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS, was used for scoped bitstream emission behavior.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Overlayed hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70 with HSDJpegWork pointers instead of using raw byte-buffer arithmetic.
Replaced repeated bit write/flush/0xFF-stuffing code with HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS inside hsd_803B3CD8.
Used table pointer selection for luminance/chrominance paths: dc_code/dc_size and ac_code/ac_size chosen from lbl_80431678/lbl_8043169C/lbl_80431090 offsets.
Rewrote repeated row and column transform code as two 8-iteration loops and a 64-element shift loop.
Used hsd_803B3344 and hsd_803B3398 helper calls for JPEG marker and block writes instead of open-coded buffer checks in some places.
Caution from this PR: these tactics improved readability but did not preserve matching in the submitted form; verify each tactic against codegen and data order before reuse.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] GALE01 report: matched code 67.18% (-32 bytes) and matched data 39.32% (-1104 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] reported 2 broken matches: main/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34 .data dropped from 100.00% to 52.77% (-522 bytes), and hsd_803B5C2C dropped from 100.00% to 0.00% (-32 bytes).
decomp-dev[bot] listed 2 .sdata2 improvements: hsd_3B5C .sdata2 +22 bytes and hsd_3B34 .sdata2 +13 bytes.
decomp-dev[bot] listed regressions in hsd_803B3CD8, hsd_803B51C8, hsd_803B46D4, hsd_803B4A2C, hsd_803B3408, hsd_803B6BE4, fn_803B376C, fn_803B61B4, fn_803B6820, hsd_803B5EA0, and hsd_803B4D64.
PR metadata shows draft=true, merged=false, and closed_at 2026-05-18T18:25:52Z.
follow_up_queries: Search later accepted PRs for HSDJpegWork or hsd_3B34 JPEG work before reusing this draft's struct layout.
Re-audit hsd_804D2648 and hsd_804D2E70 offsets against assembly before replacing M2C_FIELD uses with HSDJpegWork fields.
Check table placement/staticness for lbl_80430C40, lbl_80430C80, lbl_80431090, and lbl_80431638; decomp.dev reported hsd_3B34 .data breakage.
Test whether HSD_JPEG_EMIT_BITS-style factoring can match MWCC, or whether the bit emission must stay unrolled/open-coded.
Validate prototype changes for hsd_803B5C4C, hsd_803B5D70, hsd_803B5EA0, hsd_803B51C8, and hsd_803B6BE4 against callsites and generated assembly.
If reviving this work, split it into separate PRs for data tables, header/struct layout, encoder code, and decoder code to isolate regressions.
